---
title: "Volatility Breakout Final Project"
jupyter: python3
---

::: {.hero-banner}
## Developing and Backtesting a Volatility Breakout Strategy

This project is a FINTECH 533 final-project scaffold for a volatility and channel breakout strategy. It is organized as a Quarto website backed by an `analysis/` Python package and a `shinybroker`-compatible research pipeline.
:::

```{python}
from analysis.breakout_strategy import run_analysis

analysis = run_analysis()
overview = analysis["overview"]
metrics = analysis["metrics_display"]
overview
```

## Project Scope

- Build a transparent breakout strategy with explicit entry and exit rules.
- Backtest the strategy on historical price data using a repeatable Python workflow.
- Log every trade so entries, exits, and holding periods are auditable.
- Extend the baseline strategy with a supervised learning filter for breakout quality.

## Strategy Snapshot

The current project setup targets liquid ETFs and focuses on volatility expansion after price leaves a recent channel. The site and package are structured to support both a baseline breakout engine and a later model-based filter that decides which breakout setups are worth taking.

## What To Review

- [Asset Selection](asset-selection.qmd) describes the planned universe and screening logic.
- [Methodology](methodology.qmd) documents the trading rules, assumptions, and intended implementation choices.
- [Results](results.qmd) is where equity growth, drawdown, and performance statistics will appear.
- [Trade Log](trade-log.qmd) will hold the blotter, ledger preview, and download links.
- [Monitoring](monitoring.qmd) defines how to judge whether the strategy is still behaving as expected after deployment.
